hard.next_step: What We Covered Today Merlin Intake & Agentic Sourcing — end-to-end RFP creation via Microsoft Teams, AI-generated SOWs, price benchmarking and supplier recommendations iContract — metadata extraction, clause library, Instareview, bulk amendment (in beta, targeted ~6 months), obligation tracking and SearchGenie Supplier Onboarding & iRisk — D&B/Moody's integrations, configurable onboarding questionnaires, supplier risk radar Guided Walkthrough Session Following your feedback, we'd love to set up a focused, hands-on session where your broader team can experience the guided buying journey, approval flows and end-to-end process first-hand. We'd like to propose the following options, for a 1-hour timeslot. Monday 29 June, 4:00 – 5:00 PM Tuesday 30 June, 11:00 AM – 1:00 PM RFP call check reviewed, no change - Final demo on the 22nd - decision 2 weeks after that - we are preferred vendor - confirmed on call - timelines for contracting is July Submitted RFP on the 3rd June - we will hear back- The responses should arrive no later than 3 weeks after receipt of the RFP Pricing to be finialised 01/06 RFP recieved deadline 4th of June RFP is a carbon copy of Zycus S2P Primer! Meeting with Anna-Marie (CTO) went well - Confirmed we demo'd the best, our platform meet their needs and wants a partnership,believe's - we can meet those objective
hard.ais_why: Engaged across AI features—Merlin intake and 'Merlin risk radar' with '19 credible sources' and alerting; senior sponsor: 'I like the interface... I like the team's integration.'	Explicit AI-reliability concern tempers excitement: 'everybody that has worked with AI knows that sometimes it gives you an answer that is not actually correct, but is telling you it's correct.'	Strong commercial momentum (RFP out, proposal requested, budget aligned) but AI interest is feature-level, not a stated agentic mandate. Three transcripts—high confidence.
hard.primary_competitor: Do Nothing / Delay (SteerCo fairness re-score; no named rival actively preferred)

north_star_verdict.headline: Live, healthy late-mid deal: Global Switch has confirmed Zycus as the preferred vendor on a full-suite S2P award and their RFP was effectively a copy of the Zycus S2P primer, with a detailed walkthrough set for 6 Jul and contracting flagged for July, but the verbal preference is not yet a formal award and the external Shoosmiths MSA redline (2-3 weeks) against a 30 Jul close makes the date tight, so signature likely slips into August.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 2026-07-06. Close 2026-07-30 (24 days out). In Shortlisted since 2026-06-15 (~3 weeks in stage), Qualified 2026-03-27, created 2026-03-18. Path remaining: 6 Jul detailed walkthrough and SteerCo award decision, then MSA redline via external counsel Shoosmiths 2-3 weeks (Martin Loveday, 2026-06-15) landing ~20-27 Jul, then ~2 weeks signing. That sequence does not fit inside 24 days, so the 30 Jul date will likely slip into August. Deal is engaged: LastActivityDate 2026-07-02, buyer email 2026-06-29 asking to meet Monday 6 Jul, 8+ person buyer demo 2026-06-24.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Best Case (defensible; buyer-confirmed preferred vendor, strong champion, live engagement - the only open risks are date credibility and converting verbal preference into a signed formal award)
evidence: Next Step log: 'Final demo on the 22nd - decision 2 weeks after that - we are preferred vendor - confirmed on call - timelines for contracting is July.'
Next Step log: 'Meeting with Anna-Marie went well - Confirmed we demo'd the best, our platform meet their needs and wants a partnership.'
Martin Loveday, 2026-06-15: Zycus is a front runner; the RFP was a carbon copy of the Zycus S2P primer.
Martin Loveday, 2026-06-15 on legal: no in-house counsel, will use external firm Shoosmiths, redline will take a couple of weeks (critical-path timing risk vs 30 Jul).
Gavin Greer email 2026-06-29: 'could we do the detailed run through next Monday, 6th July?' (buyer-pull; engagement pulse LIVE).

metrics.narrative: The business problem is well established and quoted: Gavin Greer (2026-03-17) said procurement is 'fairly manual... if we could have written on paper, we'd probably still be there', with a standalone system that 'is literally just a way of raising a PO to get suppliers paid.' Scale is quantified: ~£180m spend and ~25,000 POs a year, ~350 users, ~200 contracts plus ~50 new per year (Martin Loveday and buyer, 2026-06-15). Zycus quantified the value lever (Anthony Gray, 2026-03-17): Merlin Intake lifts requisition adoption from 47% to 93% and cuts sourcing-to-PO cycle time by up to ~60%. What is still NOT quantified is Global Switch's own baseline cycle time - Gavin (2026-03-17): 'step one is what are our cycle times. So we don't have anything in place.' Converting the ~60% claim into a Global-Switch-specific savings/ROI number is the open move.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-03-17 (Gavin Greer, Anthony Gray)

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with Anna-Marie Ferguson (Director of Business Intelligence and Performance), the senior sponsor whose sign-off the deal team is chasing: the 2026-07-02 task reads 'through Martin, confirm the proposed number meets Anna-Marie's budget', and the Next Step log records 'Meeting with Anna-Marie went well - confirmed we demo'd the best... wants a partnership.' Procurement decision ownership sits with Gavin Greer (Group Procurement Director), who runs the RFP, drives the SteerCo fairness re-score, and asked for the 6 Jul detailed run-through (email 2026-06-29). The anomaly and open risk is that the final number is being relayed to Anna-Marie via champion Martin Loveday rather than closed directly with her, so budget sign-off is confirmed in sentiment but not in writing. The move is to broker a direct final-number confirmation through Martin ahead of the award.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicit and quoted from the structured use-case demo (2026-06-24), built around three buyer-supplied use cases (a 5-year UPS maintenance contract, a generator-set strategic sourcing event, and a consultancy SOW). Confirmed criteria: ease of use and low change-management for non-procurement users (Chris Richards asked for 'a slower walk through' so a 'non-procurement person can go into the system and really be taken through step by step'); end-to-end agentic sourcing with auto-generated RFPs and SOWs plus price benchmarking; contracting depth (metadata extraction, clause library, Instareview, redlining, Certinal/DocuSign e-sign); supplier onboarding and risk (D&B/Moody's, Risk Radar 19 sources); multi-currency global support (Anna-Marie: pricing in Singapore/Hong Kong/London currencies at FX). Full-suite breadth is a hard criterion - the buyer dismissed cheaper rivals as too thin ('only got like 3 modules, which we don't think is gonna cover what we need').
sources: Avoma demo 2026-06-24 (Chris Richards, Anna-Marie Ferguson, Gavin Greer)

decision_process.narrative: The process is legible from the call sequence: intro (2026-03-17) with Gavin and Martin, discovery (2026-04-07), check-ins (2026-05-27, 2026-06-15), then the structured use-case demo (2026-06-24) that pulled in the full committee - Gavin Greer (procurement), Anna-Marie Ferguson (finance/BI), Chris Richards (service delivery), Stefanie Knevel (contracts), plus Martin - signalling committee-wide evaluation. The buyer's stated path: RFP submitted 3 Jun, responses within 3 weeks, final demo on the 22nd, decision ~2 weeks after, then a SteerCo award and July contracting. Gavin drives a fairness re-score of the other vendors before the formal award and requested the 6 Jul detailed run-through. Approval chain runs through Anna-Marie for budget and Gavin/SteerCo for the procurement award.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partly mapped and are the main date risk. Global Switch has no in-house counsel and will use external firm Shoosmiths, whose MSA redline Martin Loveday (2026-06-15) flagged will 'take a couple of weeks' - the critical-path item against a 30 Jul close. Stefanie Knevel (Procurement Manager Contracts, Group) is the contracts owner. The buyer uses DocuSign today; Zycus positioned its sister-company e-sign tool Certinal at ~one third to one quarter of DocuSign cost (Nishita, 2026-06-24). As a full-suite new-business S2P deal this is an MSA plus Order Form plus SOW motion; the SOW is the likely signature predictor. Martin flagged that the legal team missed the 24 Jun demo and 'that may be a key part we wanna cover off', so a legal/contracting-specific session is the visibility move.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific, multi-owner and quoted. Core pain (Gavin Greer, 2026-03-17): fully manual procurement with a PO-only system and no cycle-time visibility. Legal/contracting pain is acute: Gavin (2026-06-24) 'people sort of send an email with a contract and say please review this... legal team have got no context to go along with it', and manual metadata entry is a stated error source. Adoption pain for non-procurement end users is owned by Chris Richards, who wants a guided step-by-step journey. Data-quality pain: the buyer is unsure how well catalogs are maintained today. Supplier financial-health checks are painful ('we have a really ridiculous process for that at the moment' - Anna-Marie, 2026-06-24). Pain owners: Gavin (procurement/legal), Anna-Marie (finance/BI reporting), Chris (adoption), Stefanie (contracts).
sources: Avoma demo 2026-06-24 (Gavin Greer, Anna-Marie Ferguson, Chris Richards)

champion.narrative: Martin Loveday (Global Switch Product Owner, reports to Chris Richards) is a strong, active, multi-threaded champion who authored the RFP off the Zycus primer, runs the cadence with Zycus, openly shares competitive standing ('you're a front runner for us', 2026-06-15), volunteers internal process and timelines, and coordinated the use-case pack for the 24 Jun demo. He is the access path to power - both Anna-Marie (budget) and Gavin (procurement award) engage Zycus through him, and the final number is being relayed via the relevant stakeholder watch item: advocacy at finance and legal is newer (they joined on 2026-06-24 and asked for a slower walkthrough), and Martin was on leave around 26 Jun, so arm him to extend the champion base to Chris/Stefanie/legal ahead of the award and ensure coverage when he is out.

competition.narrative: Zycus is the buyer-confirmed preferred vendor ('we are preferred vendor - confirmed on call', Next Step log) and scored near-perfect on an RFP Martin Loveday described as a 'carbon copy' of the Zycus S2P primer, giving a strong structural advantage. GEP and Coupa are named in the CRM competitive field but were not actively pressed in recent calls; the buyer praised Merlin Intake as the best UI they have seen. The live competitive threat is not a named rival but lower-cost, thin-scope S2P vendors (Martin, 2026-06-15: 'some are cheaper, but it looks like they've only got like 3 modules... not gonna cover what we need'), one of which 'hasn't even put in an actual implementation cost', plus the SteerCo fairness re-score and a do-nothing/delay drift against a tight close. The wedge to defend: full-suite breadth plus the agentic Merlin Intake advantage the buyer already prefers.

deal_movement.summary: Deal accelerated in mid-June and is engaged today: amount and stage stepped up on 2026-06-15, a full-suite committee demo landed on 2026-06-24, and the buyer named Zycus preferred vendor with July contracting. Last real two-way touch was Gavin Greer's 2026-06-29 email pulling a 6 Jul detailed walkthrough (LastActivityDate 2026-07-02).

items.change: Amount raised from £150k to £234k as the deal moved to full S2P suite scope (Claire Hudson).
Stage advanced Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted; close date reset 30 Jun to 30 Jul (Claire Hudson).
Full-suite use-case demo delivered to the buying committee (Gavin, Anna-Marie, Chris, Stefanie, Martin).
Anna-Marie Ferguson confirmed Zycus demo'd the best, meets their needs, and wants a partnership.
Buyer confirmed Zycus as preferred vendor with contracting flagged for July (Next Step log).
Gavin Greer emailed asking for the detailed run-through on Monday 6 Jul (buyer-pull).

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the buyer-confirmed preferred vendor on a full-suite S2P award, structurally advantaged because the RFP was effectively a copy of the Zycus S2P primer. No named rival is actively preferred: GEP and Coupa sit in the CRM field but were not pressed in recent calls, and the buyer praised Merlin Intake as the best UI they have seen. The strongest CURRENT threats are not a named vendor but (1) the SteerCo fairness re-score of cheaper, thin-scope vendors before the formal award, and (2) do-nothing/delay drift against a tight close - both neutralised by converting the verbal preference into a dated, signed award. Cheaper 3-module rivals are dismissed as functionally inadequate and one self-disqualified by not submitting an implementation cost.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Delay
competitors.quote: We want to make sure we do the right things... we are taking our time to make sure we get this right.
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor to the buyer's July contracting and year-end go-live timeline; use the 6 Jul walkthrough to lock a dated award and keep urgency.

competitors.name: Lower-cost thin S2P vendor(s) (~3 modules)
competitors.quote: Some are cheaper, but it looks like they've only got like 3 modules, which we don't think is gonna cover what we need at all.
competitors.how_we_win: Hold the line on full-suite breadth and total cost of ownership; show 3-module rivals cannot deliver the end-to-end guided journey and Risk Radar the buyer needs.

competitors.name: Non-responsive S2P vendor (no implementation cost submitted)
competitors.quote: Someone hasn't even put in an actual implementation cost yet... if they can't be bothered to do that, then no.
competitors.how_we_win: Vendor self-disqualified by incomplete RFP; emphasise Zycus's complete, costed proposal and delivery confidence.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: GEP, Coupa - we have a better AI platform leading with Merlin Intake, in their words is the best UI they have seen.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with the agentic Merlin Intake UI advantage the buyer already praised; reinforce full-suite parity.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: GEP, Coupa - we have a better AI platform leading with Merlin Intake.
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on agentic AI and usability; not actively pressed in recent calls.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is strong on the criteria that matter most to Global Switch - full-suite breadth, agentic sourcing/contracting automation, and multi-currency global support - with a confirmed 'you demo'd the best' from Anna-Marie. The one exposed area is legal/contracting depth and non-procurement-user adoption, where the buyer wants a slower hands-on walkthrough and a legal-specific session before contracting.

best_practice_check.summary: The win is close but hinges on converting a verbal preference into a signed award and de-risking the external-counsel contracting path before 30 Jul.
flags: Use Monday's 6 Jul walkthrough to convert Gavin Greer's verbal preferred-vendor status into a dated, written formal award - the single highest-leverage lever now.
De-risk the close date: engage Stefanie Knevel and Global Switch's external counsel Shoosmiths early on the MSA/SOW, since a 2-3 week external redline is the critical path against 30 Jul.
Multi-thread into the finance and legal reviewers who joined late on 24 Jun and asked for a slower walkthrough, so advocacy extends beyond Martin Loveday before the award.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strongly positioned: the buyer named Merlin Intake the best UI they have seen and engaged deeply with agentic sourcing, auto-SOW generation, and iContract InstaReview during the 24 Jun demo. Positioning is landing at feature level; the residual gap is the AI-reliability concern that tempers full agentic trust.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 / Moderate is well-calibrated to the calls: real, feature-level AI appetite (Merlin Intake, agentic RFP/SOW creation, Risk Radar's 19 sources, InstaReview) but tempered by an explicit AI-reliability concern and no stated agentic mandate. Interest is genuine but not a transformation-scale AI mandate.

Verbal preferred-vendor status is not yet a formal award; Gavin Greer is running a SteerCo fairness re-score of other vendors first.

Chris Richards flagged that non-procurement users need a slower, step-by-step guided journey before they are comfortable adopting the platform.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and buyer-pulled: Gavin requested the 6 Jul walkthrough, Anna-Marie endorsed the platform, and the committee is broad. No cooling signal.

customer_preference.evidence: Next Step log: 'we are preferred vendor - confirmed on call' and Anna-Marie 'Confirmed we demo'd the best, our platform meet their needs and wants a partnership'; buyer praised Merlin Intake as the best UI they have seen.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain and a ~60% cycle-time reduction claim are established, but Global Switch has no baseline cycle time measured, so no Global-Switch-specific ROI/savings figure is confirmed.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We are the buyer-confirmed preferred vendor on a full-suite S2P award, with a 6 Jul detailed walkthrough and a July contracting timeline; the only things between us and the win are converting Gavin Greer's verbal preference into a signed award and clearing the external Shoosmiths MSA redline before 30 Jul.

win_position.text: Shortlisted caps confidence, but this earns a high-70s win read because the buyer confirmed Zycus as preferred vendor on a full suite the cheaper 3-module rivals cannot match, and the RFP was a carbon copy of the Zycus S2P primer.
Anna-Marie Ferguson confirmed on 24 Jun that Zycus demo'd the best and wants a partnership, and the buyer praised Merlin Intake as the best UI they have seen.
Held below the low-80s because the preference is still verbal, not a signed award, and Gavin Greer is running a SteerCo fairness re-score of other vendors first (2026-06-24).
The final number is being relayed to budget owner Anna-Marie via Martin rather than closed directly, so budget sign-off is not yet in writing (2026-07-02).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is strong and buyer-pulled: Gavin Greer emailed on 2026-06-29 asking for the detailed run-through Monday 6 Jul, with the committee (procurement, finance/BI, service delivery, contracts) engaged on the 24 Jun demo.
The buyer named concrete dates - RFP responses within 3 weeks of 3 Jun, decision ~2 weeks after the 22nd demo, and July contracting - so the close plan is concretizing.
Several Zycus deliverables are overdue (demo recording due 27 Jun, EB proposal prep), so cadence discipline is needed to keep pace toward the award.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is high: the buyer stated Zycus is the preferred vendor and pulled the next meeting themselves, and Anna-Marie signalled a partnership intent.
Commitment is not yet contractual - no formal award, no written budget sign-off, and legal has not engaged, so the verbal lead must be converted to paper.

deal_risk.text: The 30 Jul close is at risk because external counsel Shoosmiths needs 2-3 weeks for the MSA redline plus signing, which does not fit the remaining 24 days.
Non-procurement-user adoption (Chris Richards wants a slower guided walkthrough) and the unengaged legal team are the residual delivery risks to clear before contracting.

close_plan.evidence: Buyer named decision and July contracting dates and pulled the 6 Jul walkthrough, but no signed mutual close plan yet.

timeline_risk.evidence: External Shoosmiths MSA redline (2-3 weeks) plus signing does not fit before 30 Jul; date likely slips to August.

scope_change.to: £234k full S2P suite (iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA)
scope_change.detail: Amount rose from £150k to £234k on 2026-06-15 as the deal moved to the full source-to-pay suite; scope expanded rather than narrowed, a confidence signal.

reasons.detail: The demo recording and outstanding follow-up materials owed to champion Martin Loveday and the broader Global Switch team were due 2026-06-27 and remain unshipped, being re-logged as an open action on 2026-07-02. This is our deliverable, not buyer-dependent, on a $234K Best Case forecasted deal where Zycus is front-runner into a tight 30-Jul close. Letting our own follow-up drift gives the SteerCo fairness re-score and 'do nothing/delay' factions room to stall the award.
reasons.metric: demo recording 5+ days overdue (due Jun 27, still open Jul 2)
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Claire why the demo recording and follow-up pack to Martin Loveday is still unsent 5+ days past its Jun 27 due date, and whether the Jul 6 guided-walkthrough date is locked.
reasons.summary: Demo recording to champion Martin overdue ~6 days, still open
reasons.evidence: our_open_deliverables: 'Share the demo recording and any outstanding follow-up materials with Martin Loveday and the broader Global Switch team.' due 2026-06-27, status overdue; re-logged as an open task on 2026-07-02.

critical_signals.text: Global Switch confirmed Zycus as the preferred vendor after the 24 Jun demo, and their RFP was effectively a copy of the Zycus S2P primer, so we hold a strong structural lead with no rival actively preferred.

The 24 Jun full-suite demo landed well and Anna-Marie Ferguson said we demo'd the best and want a partnership, though Chris Richards asked for a slower hands-on walkthrough for non-procurement users, booked for 6 Jul.

Pricing was finalised in early June but the final number is being relayed to budget owner Anna-Marie Ferguson through Martin rather than closed directly, so budget sign-off is not yet confirmed in writing.
Group Procurement Director Gavin Greer is now driving the decision and a fairness re-score of other vendors before the formal award, reached through champion Martin Loveday.
The award is expected around 6 Jul but the MSA must go through Global Switch's external counsel Shoosmiths (2-3 weeks), so the 30 Jul close will likely slip into August.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 70 is carried by momentum 73 vs stage-expected 56; preference strength +1.00 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 73 (moving forward): seniority rising (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 64 is anchored on the stage win (70), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 73, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: We are the buyer-confirmed preferred vendor on a full-suite S2P award, with a 6 Jul detailed walkthrough and a July contracting timeline; the only things between us and the win are converting Gavin Greer's verbal preference into a signed award and clearing the external Shoosmiths MSA redline before 30 Jul.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Shortlisted caps confidence, but this earns a high-70s win read because the buyer confirmed Zycus as preferred vendor on a full suite the cheaper 3-module rivals cannot match, and the RFP was a carbon copy of the Zycus S2P primer.
Anna-Marie Ferguson confirmed on 24 Jun that Zycus demo'd the best and wants a partnership, and the buyer praised Merlin Intake as the best UI they have seen.
Held below the low-80s because the preference is still verbal, not a signed award, and Gavin Greer is running a SteerCo fairness re-score of other vendors first (2026-06-24).
The final number is being relayed to budget owner Anna-Marie via Martin rather than closed directly, so budget sign-off is not yet in writing (2026-07-02).

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the underlying read to ~75 — but we cap confidence at 70 until the stage advances and sign-off lands. Read the gap as 'earned, but not yet banked.'

bullets.text: Momentum is strong and buyer-pulled: Gavin Greer emailed on 2026-06-29 asking for the detailed run-through Monday 6 Jul, with the committee (procurement, finance/BI, service delivery, contracts) engaged on the 24 Jun demo.
The buyer named concrete dates - RFP responses within 3 weeks of 3 Jun, decision ~2 weeks after the 22nd demo, and July contracting - so the close plan is concretizing.
Several Zycus deliverables are overdue (demo recording due 27 Jun, EB proposal prep), so cadence discipline is needed to keep pace toward the award.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: The 30 Jul close is at risk because external counsel Shoosmiths needs 2-3 weeks for the MSA redline plus signing, which does not fit the remaining 24 days.
Non-procurement-user adoption (Chris Richards wants a slower guided walkthrough) and the unengaged legal team are the residual delivery risks to clear before contracting.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Zycus is the buyer-confirmed preferred vendor on a full-suite S2P award, structurally advantaged because the RFP was effectively a copy of the Zycus S2P primer.

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: Commitment is high: the buyer stated Zycus is the preferred vendor and pulled the next meeting themselves, and Anna-Marie signalled a partnership intent.
Commitment is not yet contractual - no formal award, no written budget sign-off, and legal has not engaged, so the verbal lead must be converted to paper.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Use Monday's walkthrough to secure Gavin Greer's written formal award and lock July contracting. (by 8 Jul)
Broker final-number confirmation against Anna-Marie Ferguson's budget through Martin Loveday. (by 10 Jul)
Book a legal-specific contracting session with Stefanie Knevel and Global Switch's Shoosmiths counsel. (by 15 Jul)
